scope: Survey of representation shapes so readers don't assume every representation is a flat matrix.
key_concepts: Not all data can or should be forced into a flat tabular matrix (X in R^(nxd)).
Different phenomena naturally require different mathematical structures (topologies).
Key structures: Flat Vectors/Matrices (tabular), Sequences (time series, text), Grids/Tensors (images), Graphs (networks/relationships), and Sets (unordered items).

definitions.definition: Data where the order of elements carries meaning.
A structure representing entities (nodes) and their relationships (edges).

examples: A standard user profile works well as a flat vector.
A sentence or a stock price history is a sequence; reversing the order ruins the meaning.
A social network is a graph, where the connection between users is the primary signal.

relations.builds_toward: Part IV (Feature Engineering by Data Type)

exclusions: Do not explain the mathematics of tensors or graph neural networks. Keep it to structural intuition.
